name: Cadrenal Therapeutics, Inc.
business: Cadrenal Therapeutics is a late-stage biopharmaceutical company advancing novel therapies for life-threatening immune and thrombotic conditions. Its lead product candidate, CAD-1005, is a first-in-class selective 12-LOX inhibitor being developed to treat heparin-induced thrombocytopenia (HIT). The broader pipeline includes two additional clinical-stage assets: tecarfarin, an oral vitamin K antagonist for chronic anticoagulation in patients with kidney dysfunction or left ventricular assist devices, and frunexian, an intravenous Factor XIa inhibitor for acute care settings.
sellsTo: patients with ESKD and atrial fibrillation

exposures.sentence: We are a late-stage biopharmaceutical company advancing novel therapies for life-threatening immune and thrombotic conditions.

catalysts.event: End-of-Phase 2 meeting with FDA completed, clarifying a potential registrational path for Phase 3 trial of CAD-1005
catalysts.sentence: On March 26, 2026, we completed our End-of-Phase 2 (EOP2) meeting with the FDA and clarified a potential registrational path for our planned Phase 3 pivotal trial of CAD-1005 in patients with HIT.

catalysts.event: Completion of manufacturing of tecarfarin drug product in accordance with cGMP
catalysts.sentence: We have recently completed the manufacturing of tecarfarin drug product in accordance with current good manufacturing practices (cGMP).

catalysts.event: Phase 3 trial protocol subject to additional information from FDA meeting minutes and further comments
catalysts.sentence: Our Phase 3 trial protocol will still be subject to additional information which may be set forth in the final meeting minutes from the FDA and any further comments we may receive from the FDA upon their review of the protocol.

tamClaims.claim: Peak annual market revenue potential for CAD-1005 in patients with HIT is approximately $825 million.
tamClaims.sentence: Based upon management's analysis of market research and external data, and assuming that we receive FDA approval of CAD-1005, we estimate that the peak annual market revenue potential for CAD-1005 in patients with HIT will be approximately $825 million.

tamClaims.claim: Combined peak annual U.S. market revenue potential for tecarfarin and frunexian in patients with orphan and high-risk cardiovascular conditions is approximately $2 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: Based upon management's analysis of market research and external data, and assuming that we receive FDA approval of tecarfarin and frunexian, we estimate that the combined peak annual U.S. market revenue potential for these product candidates in patients with orphan and high-risk cardiovascular conditions is approximately $2 billion.
